summary: Core PowerShell module and workflow structure are well-designed with proper error handling (Set-StrictMode, explicit error messages), idempotent steps, least-privilege permissions, and good CI/CD patterns. License compliance logic is correct (deny wins over allow). However, test harness references `.actrc` file (line 67, Invoke-ActTests.ps1) without providing it—Copy-Item would fail immediately. Global state via `$script:LicenseDb` for mocking is functional but fragile. Fixture paths hardcoded in workflow without validation they exist; test fixtures embedded in harness script rather than separate files. Regex patterns (requirements.txt parsing, version stripping) lack explanatory comments. Missing .actrc definition blocks successful test execution.
